headline: How France's far-right RN lost a longstanding northern stronghold in the municipal elections
description: Villers-Cotterêts, a northern French town of 10,000 residents, had been governed by far-right mayor Franck Briffaut for 12 years. On March 22, independent centrist Jeanne Roussel took the town from the far right with 52.51% of the vote.

Russian barrage pounds Kyiv, killing at least 24
A history of failed negotiations between Israel and Lebanon
description: In 1983, a peace agreement was signed between Israel and Lebanon but the Lebanese government annulled it just a few months later under pressure from Syria. Further bilateral negotiations took place between 1991 and 1993, following the Madrid Peace Conference, but yielded no results.

headline: School uniforms have 'limited effects on student behavior and learning,' reveals French experiment
description: The experiment with a 'standard uniform,' launched in 2023, has involved about 100 elementary, middle and high schools. The first study on the measure highlights significant differences in perception between adults and students, and found 'modest and mixed' impacts on the school environment.

headline: 2026 World Cup: France reveals 26-player squad
description: Coach Didier Deschamps on Thursday announced his squad for this summer's tournament in the United States, Canada and Mexico. Kylian Mbappé will likely captain the team despite a thigh injury sustained last month.

headline: Orphaned lynx released into the wild: The fight to save France's wildcats
description: The lynx, which disappeared from France at the beginning of the 20th century, is threatened by low genetic diversity, habitat fragmentation, road collisions and illegal killings.

How China is cracking down on its Christians
description: Le Monde verified footage from Chinese social media and collected testimonies from victims of Beijing's crackdown to reveal how the regime has brought independent churches to heel since Xi Jinping came to power.

headline: Wine consumption continues to decline around the world
description: Wine consumption fell by 2.7% year-on-year, according to data released on Tuesday by the International Organization of Vine and Wine. But France remains the world's leading wine exporter by value.

headline: The shattered lives of Iranians Manije, Mohammad-Reza and Soroush, all killed by bombings
description: Before the Israeli-US bombings began, they were living as retirees and artists in different parts of Tehran. Until they met their deaths in their own homes. Tormented by distress, their loved ones abroad and in Iran recount their last conversations before fate took a tragic turn.
